_comment: Compiled representation of job_description.md for Senior AI Engineer - Founding Team @ Redrob AI. This is hand-authored from a careful reading of the JD, not auto-extracted, because the JD explicitly rewards reasoning about gap between what it says and what it means.
jd_text_for_embedding: Senior AI Engineer Founding Team Redrob AI talent intelligence platform. Own the intelligence layer: ranking, retrieval, and matching systems that decide what recruiters see when they search for candidates. Production experience with embeddings-based retrieval systems (sentence-transformers, OpenAI embeddings, BGE, E5) deployed to real users, handling embedding drift, index refresh, retrieval-quality regression in production. Production experience with vector databases or hybrid search infrastructure: Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, OpenSearch, Elasticsearch, FAISS. Strong Python, code quality matters. Hands-on experience designing evaluation frameworks for ranking systems: NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-to-online correlation, A/B test interpretation. Has shipped at least one end-to-end ranking, search, or recommendation system to real users at meaningful scale. Strong opinions on retrieval (hybrid vs dense), evaluation (offline vs online), LLM integration (fine-tune vs prompt). Scrappy product-engineering attitude, ships fast, owns the full stack from infra to algorithms to product.

experience_band.note: Range not a hard requirement; strong candidates outside the band still considered.

location.note: Outside-India candidates are case-by-case and de-prioritized since no visa sponsorship; Tier-1 Indian city candidates or those willing to relocate are preferred.

notice_period.note: Sub-30 days ideal. 30-60 days acceptable with buyout. 60+ days raises the bar without disqualifying.

must_have_capabilities.description: Production experience with embeddings-based retrieval systems deployed to real users (not just experimentation).
Production experience with vector databases or hybrid search infrastructure.
Hands-on experience designing evaluation frameworks for ranking systems (NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-online correlation, A/B testing).

hard_disqualifiers.description: Career entirely in pure research environments (academic labs, research-only roles) with zero production deployment evidence.
All 'AI experience' is under 12 months and consists primarily of LangChain-calling-OpenAI projects, with no substantial pre-LLM-era (pre ~2022) ML/production experience.
Senior engineer who has not written production code in 18+ months because they moved into pure architecture/tech-lead/management roles.
Career trajectory shows switching companies roughly every <=1.5 years chasing title bumps (Senior -> Staff -> Principal) with no evidence of depth at any one stop.
hard_disqualifiers.detection: avg_tenure_months < 18 AND number_of_jobs >= 3 AND titles show seniority escalation

hard_disqualifiers.description: Profile/skills are dominated by trendy AI/ML keyword skills (LangChain, LoRA, GANs, fine-tuning, etc.) with little to no career_history evidence of having built or operated real systems; tutorial/demo-shaped experience.

Entire career at consulting/services firms (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Cognizant, Capgemini, etc.) with no product-company experience. Currently at one of these with PRIOR product-company experience is fine and not penalized.
Primary expertise is computer vision, speech, or robotics without significant NLP/information-retrieval exposure.
5+ years entirely on closed-source proprietary systems with zero external validation (no papers, talks, open-source, blog, public profile signal).

explicit_traps_called_out_by_jd: Do not just count AI keywords in the skills section - that is an intentional trap.
A candidate with all the right keywords as skills but an unrelated current title (e.g. Marketing Manager) is not a fit, regardless of skill list completeness.
A Tier-5-sounding candidate (plain language, no jargon) whose career_history shows they actually built a recommendation/ranking/retrieval system at a product company IS a fit.
Behavioral signals matter: a perfect-on-paper candidate inactive 6+ months with low recruiter_response_rate is not actually available for hiring purposes and should be down-weighted.
